description: Validates that all extensions enabled by default in Bluefin are loaded
and in ENABLED state. A non-1 state means the extension crashed or was
disabled — which breaks the Bluefin UX.

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI

result.error_message: ASSERT FAILED: Extension 'appindicatorsupport@rgcjonas.gmail.com' is not enabled (state=99). Expected state=1 (ENABLED).

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI

result.error_message: ASSERT FAILED: Extension 'bazaar-integration@kolunmi.github.io' is not enabled (state=6). Expected state=1 (ENABLED).

description: Validates Firefox launches and core browser UI elements are accessible.
Covers startup, navigation, tab management, and coredump regressions.
Skipped on non-Bluefin images (e.g. dakota) where Firefox is a Flatpak
and Flatpak startup latency causes suite-wide timeouts.

elements.name: Firefox launches and main window is accessible
Address bar is present and focusable

steps.name: Firefox tab count increases after Ctrl+T

description: GNOME ships built-in accessibility features. Verify the a11y
infrastructure is functional and key settings are configurable.

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI
GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI

elements.name: High contrast mode can be toggled via gsettings

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI
Run and save command output: "gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.a11y.interface high-contrast true"
Return code of last command output "is" "0"
Run and save command output: "gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.a11y.interface high-contrast"
Run and save command output: "gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.a11y.interface high-contrast false"
Return code of last command output "is" "0"

elements.name: Keyboard accessibility settings key is readable

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI
Run and save command output: "gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.a11y.keyboard enable"
Return code of last command output "is" "0"
GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI
Run and save command output: "pgrep -x at-spi-bus-launcher && echo running"

elements.name: No accessibility-related journal errors after session start

steps.name: GNOME Shell is accessible via AT-SPI
Run and save command output: "journalctl -b --no-pager -p err -g 'at-spi|orca|accessibility' | grep -v '^$' | wc -l"

name: GNOME accessibility
